item.title: Fiji cruise ship runs aground after hitting reef near 'Cast Away island'
item.description: Thirty passengers have reportedly been rescued after a cruise ship ran aground on a reef near the island where the film Cast Away was shot.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 6: Take a walk down memory lane
item.description: Where was the Venus de Milo found? And what common household item first went on sale in April 1827?

item.title: As Iran charges for access to Strait of Hormuz, world faces dangerous new era of maritime tollbooths
item.description: If Iran's idea of charging ships to pass through maritime choke points catches on, we are entering a new era of much more expensive, more dangerous global shipping.

item.title: Chinese satellite imagery of Middle East bases is helping Iran, US intelligence says
item.description: A Chinese company's publication of AI-enhanced satellite imagery of US bases in the Middle East is helping Iranian forces identify targets, US intelligence believes.

item.title: Cape Grim's 50-year air record reveals true scale of humans' impact on planet
item.description: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

item.title: Petrol station workers abused and in-store profits fall as operators deal with Middle East fuel crisis
item.description: Workers are reporting an increase in abuse from customers as service station owners say they're not making money from fuel price rises.

item.title: Zac Lomax's false start with Force adds to anticipation ahead of Super Rugby Pacific debut
item.description: Fans will have to wait for Zac Lomax's Western Force debut, providing further intrigue about how the ex-NRL star will cope when he takes his first steps in professional rugby.

item.title: How clean should a rental property be before moving in?
item.description: Having to clean a new rental property before settling in is a common experience for renters, but landlords have obligations when it comes to rental cleanliness upon moving in.

item.title: Terminal diagnosis hasn't stopped the final exhibition for Joan Humble
item.description: After a lifetime of capturing Tasmanian landscapes, Joan Humble is holding her final exhibition. The 88-year-old has a terminal cancer diagnosis, but she is determined to keep painting as long as she can hold a brush.

item.title: Dance classes providing connection, creativity and movement for older Australians
item.description: Judy Taylor discovered dance later in life, but it has now become a source of joy, energy, and purpose.

item.title: Iran rejects ceasefire proposal as intelligence chief killed in US-Israeli strikes
item.description: Iran says the intelligence chief of Iran's Revolutionary Guards, Major General Majid Khademi, was killed by US-Israeli strikes.

item.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Threats tell us more about Trump's frustration than anything else
item.description: It's day 37 of the war in the Middle East. The ABC's Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran is in Jerusalem, where Donald Trump's threats to obliterate Iran are starting to feel like business as usual.

item.title: Youth detention watchdog calls for upgrade to 'harmful' cells to improve children's access to justice
item.description: South Australia's Youth Court cells do not meet "acceptable minimum standards of safety, dignity and care", resulting in child detainees increasingly appearing in court via video link, the state's youth detention watchdog says.

item.title: Commuters swap the car for e-bikes as fuel prices soar amid Middle East war
item.description: As high fuel prices continue to affect motorists, Australians are turning to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers reporting a surge in sales.

item.title: Hundreds of flying fox pups starving to death in wake of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Rescuers are scrambling to save as many flying fox pups as they can, with hundreds of the native bats dead or dying in Top End streets.

item.title: 'Easter bilby' populations quadruple across Australian sanctuaries
item.description: The Australian Wildlife Conservancy says the threatened species has rebounded at multiple sites across the country.

item.title: Feral animals, biosecurity and careers on agenda for SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Rangers
item.description: The first SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Ranger Conference has brought together those on the front line of the fight against feral animals and plants.

item.title: Tim David stars in Royal Challengers Bengaluru's win over Chennai Super Kings
item.description: It is a mixed day for the Australians in the Indian Premier League, with Tim David powering RCB to a victory, while Travis Head fails to make an impact in Sunrisers Hyderabad's loss in Lucknow.

item.title: From Sway to Red Sunset: Bic Runga's return to music reflects where she's at
item.description: "There were definitely times where I thought I was never going to make anything again," the New Zealand star says of new album Red Sunset.

item.title: Body found after house fire in Devonport, in north-west Tasmania
item.description: A woman in her 60s is believed to be the victim of a fatal house fire in Tasmania's north-west early this morning, firefighters say.

item.title: Man found dead after crossing floodwaters on foot in SA's far north
item.description: Police have found the body of a man in his 60s who was swept away trying to cross a flooded road on foot in SA's far north over the Easter weekend.

item.title: Sydney Royal Easter Show helps emerging artists build their careers
item.description: Horse drawings and handcrafted ceramics from local artists and artisans are among the arts and crafts you can find at the Sydney Royal Easter Show.

item.title: Australia's fuel shipments secured 'well into' May, Energy Minister Chris Bowen says
item.description: Fuel shipments to Australia have been secured "well into" May, the energy minister says, as the number of service stations with diesel and petrol shortages fell over the Easter weekend.

item.title: Artist shares origin story of Field of Light installation at Uluru
item.description: Bruce Munro's art installation is inspired by Uluru. Ten years later, it remains one of the most popular attractions at the famous landmark.

item.title: Artemis II crew about to smash record for the farthest humans have ventured from Earth
item.description: The Apollo 13 crew has held the distance record for a crewed aircraft's maximum range from Earth since the 1970s. The Artemis II crew is about to change that.

item.title: How US forces pulled off daring rescue of stranded airman shot down in Iran
item.description: After an American fighter jet was shot down in a mountainous area of Iran, the US military pulled off an incredible rescue of two airmen, living up to its credo of "no man left behind", despite running into difficulty.

item.title: Daniel Gene Murray refused bail over alleged shooting inside Corrimal home
item.description: A man who allegedly stormed into his sister's home on a quiet street and started shooting has been refused bail.

item.title: 'I hate points': Gunston's last-second behind helps Hawthorn claim thriller
item.description: Hawthorn's Jack Gunston kicks a behind in the dying seconds to defeat Geelong by one point in an Easter Monday classic.

item.title: Three Italian-inspired sausage recipes
item.description: If you want some new ways to use sausages, these three easy Italian-inspired recipes will get you excited about them.

item.title: Marshall questions penalty against Tigers despite Eels star's ugly ankle injury
item.description: As the Eels grapple with another potentially season-ending injury to Bailey Simonsson, Wests Tigers coach Benji Marshall says he is not even sure the tackle that led to it was worth a penalty.

item.title: George Bailey discusses Australia's future, and present, on the ABC Cricket Podcast
item.description: Australia's aging men's cricket side is not about to "drop off" in performance, but the chairman of selectors says expectations should be managed as the squad goes through a change in the near future.

item.title: Michael Maguire faces first as Broncos coach amid injury crisis
item.description: Brisbane coach Michael Maguire is facing a brand new selection challenge against North Queensland with Ben Hunt, Adam Reynolds and Reece Walsh set to be absent at the same time.

item.title: Byron bounces back after Bluesfest cancellation as Easter crowds enjoy musical treats
item.description: There was no Bluesfest, but thousands of music fans still packed venues across the Byron Shire over the Easter long weekend.

item.title: Decades of economic decline has 'broken' Britons, pushing one fifth of people into poverty and towards political Reform
item.description: London. It's the epicentre of Britain but without it the UK's GDP is equivalent to the poorest state in the US. And one fifth of the total population is living in poverty.

item.title: Graham Arnold receives hero's welcome at Sydney Airport after Iraq World Cup qualification
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Graham Arnold mobbed by Iraqi Australians at Sydney Airport, after qualifying for World Cup
item.description: Former Socceroos coach has received a hero's welcome at Sydney Airport after helping Iraq qualify for its first FIFA World Cup in 40 years.

item.title: Essendon Bombers on cusp of record losing streak at Gather Round
item.description: Essendon cannot do anything but stay galvanised as they stare down the demoralising prospect of notching a club-record 18th consecutive defeat, coach Brad Scott has said.

item.title: Chinese AI satellite intelligence helping Iran target US forces with 'incredible precision', analysts say
item.description: Chinese satellite images enhanced by artificial intelligence could help Iran target US and allied forces to within a third of a square metre, military analysts say.

item.title: Donald Trump issues new threats against Iran
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Mollie O'Callaghan, Kyle Chalmers hail depth, Sam Short shows class on night one of Australian Open swimming
item.description: Mollie O'Callaghan and Kyle Chalmers claimed Australian Open gold medals in the 100m freestyle events, while Sam Short showed his 400m rivals a clean pair of heels on night one at the Gold Coast Aquatic Centre.

item.title: Trump warns 'whole civilisation will die' if Iran does not do a deal — as it happened
item.description: The US president took to Truth Social to issue his latest threat to Tehran, meanwhile gunman have targeted the Israeli Consulate in Istanbul.

item.title: Views from the Artemis II mission are absolutely 'unreal', astronaut says — as it happened
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before, completing a historic fly-by of the Moon.

item.title: Palestinian brothers killed by Israeli settlers in the West Bank as violence surges during Iran war
item.description: Under the cover of regional conflict, Israelis who have established illegal settlements and outposts in the West Bank become emboldened to take over more land as world attention remains on the war in Iran.

item.title: How AI is already reshaping the workforce
item.description: The wave of change from artificial intelligence rolling across the corporate landscape is going largely unnoticed as attention understandably shifts to an escalating war.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: How China uses commercial ships to pressure Taiwan without firing a shot
item.description: China has quietly mobilised thousands of fishing boats twice in recent months to form floating barriers at least 300 kilometres long.

item.title: Satellite data shows unusual Chinese fishing activity in East China Sea
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australia's major shipping lanes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Young job seekers in Australia are being targeted by crime networks as fake job scams surge
item.description: Job seekers should be wary of sharing their bank account information with potential employers, as job scams targeting young people surge, according to Scamwatch.

item.title: Farrer candidate funded by Rupert Murdoch's pro-climate son-in-law
item.description: Backers of Farrer independent candidate Michelle Milthorpe are celebrating after securing the support of a prominent pro-climate farmer who is also the son-in-law of media mogul Rupert Murdoch.

item.title: Comedians reveal how much it really costs to put on a festival show
item.description: Doing a run of shows at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival is the pinnacle for a lot of Australian comedians, but the festival's tens of thousands of punters are rarely privy to the behind-the-scenes costs.

item.title: Zein is first-generation Syrian Australian, a title that carries the weight of two worlds
item.description: For Heywire winner Zein, her family is remembered in the fragments she remembers of Syria.

item.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland coast
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology is increasingly confident the system will move towards the Queensland coast later in the week, potentially crossing Cape York Peninsula.

item.title: AACo spared investigation into cattle thirst deaths after reassuring regulators
item.description: The lack of investigation into deaths at the $700 million beef business has been described as "completely unacceptable" by a legal expert who argued industry had captured government regulators.

item.title: SA Voice members say some First Nations voters felt 'judged racially' by electoral staff
item.description: South Australia's Aboriginal Affairs Minister will consider an independent review of the state's Voice to Parliament election, following claims some voters were queried about their racial background at polling booths, while others were forced to queue for hours.

item.title: NSW truckers report increase in diesel theft amid fuel price rises
item.description: The owner of a trucking company in Western Sydney says most cases of fuel theft have occurred when his drivers have parked on the side of the road for the night.

item.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Trump's obliteration threat stands as Iran rejects truce deal
item.description: The clock is ticking down to US President Donald Trump's deadline, but Iran hasn't blinked.

item.title: Man charged with murder over stabbing at Buff Point on NSW Central Coast
item.description: A man remains in custody after facing a bail court hearing after being charged with murder over a stabbing at Buff Point, on the NSW Central Coast.

item.title: Canberra paraplegic woman stranded in hospital for 19 months due to aged care bed block
item.description: After she was stuck in a Canberra hospital for 19 months, paraplegic 84-year-old Mary Wansey has lost hope of ever finding a place in aged care.

item.title: Donald Trump says Iran could be 'taken out in one night' if deal not reached by looming deadline
item.description: Donald Trump says the "entire country" of Iran will be "taken out" if no deal is reached by Wednesday morning (AEST), ratcheting up his threat to target civilian infrastructure and brushing off concerns it would be a war crime.

item.title: WA man considering legal action after being sent home from hospital when he needed surgery
item.description: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

item.title: PVC prices soar amid petrol and gas supply issues as Iran war continues
item.description: Conflict in the Middle East has disrupted petroleum and gas supply chains, causing PVC prices to skyrocket in Australia.

item.title: Queensland maritime authorities criticised for 'slow' wrecked boats salvage
item.description: The vessel 7 Wonder has sat wrecked at Rose Bay in north Queensland for more than two months. Locals say they have been picking up rubbish from the boat daily, but maritime authorities say local salvage contractors are "stretched".

item.title: Fears for Ningaloo Reef after ex-cyclone decimates region amid coral spawning season
item.description: As sharks swarm in the aftermath, there are fears ex-tropical Cyclone Narelle may have triggered a climate disaster for the world-famous Ningaloo Reef.

item.title: Australian Army reservist faces court after allegedly working for Ukraine military without authorisation
item.description: An Australian Army reservist faces an Adelaide court after he allegedly worked for the Ukraine military as a drone operator without authorisation.

item.title: Boy dies after crash between scooter and car in Mandurah, south of Perth
item.description: An 11-year-old boy dies after his scooter collides with a car in Mandurah, with a witness saying they saw the boy riding down a small hill onto the road before the crash — WA's fourth road fatality over the Easter period.

item.title: Farmers push for stronger protections in renewable energy benefit policy
item.description: Farmers in north Queensland want a say in how renewable energy companies negotiate with councils, amid concerns poorly designed agreements could have long-term impacts on agricultural land and rural communities.

item.title: CFA firefighters who risked lives in Longwood blaze heartbroken after Easter break-in
item.description: CFA firefighters are "gutted" after thousands of dollars of equipment and fuel were stolen from Terip Terip station over the Easter long weekend.

item.title: Trump threatens to jail journalist who broke story of US rescue mission in Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump is threatening to jail the journalist who broke the story about the rescue of a downed US airman in Iran, as more details about the daring recovery mission are made public.

item.title: Council workers refuse to empty wheelie bins and issue parking fines as pay dispute intensifies
item.description: Thousands of wheelie bins in several Melbourne council areas were left unemptied on Tuesday morning as workers took industrial action over pay.

item.title: Police investigate 'suspicious' death on Barkly Highway after spate of Easter road crashes in NT
item.description: NT Police are investigating the "suspicious death" of a woman on a remote stretch of highway in the early hours of Easter morning.

item.title: Inside Delhi's growing black market for cooking gas after Strait of Hormuz closure
item.description: Across Delhi, people are waiting days and sometimes weeks for gas cylinders. Yet, the ABC has found a thriving black market, where the same gas can be bought quickly — for those who can afford it.

item.title: Don't ask me about my love life. I'm busy writing a different story
item.description: I might be on a different path, but I can still love and be loved in ways that don't require me to be a mother or a partner.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith in custody at Silverwater jail after war crimes charges
item.description: One of Australia's most decorated soldiers, Ben Roberts-Smith, has been charged with five counts of the war crime of murder.

item.title: Rohan Dennis speaks publicly for the first time since Melissa Hoskins's death
item.description: Former professional cyclist Rohan Dennis has spoken publicly for the first time about the death of his wife Melissa Hoskins, saying he and his children have since been "harassed" and "stalked" by the media.

item.title: Ex-Bondi nanny Adriana Elcira Rivas fails in bid to avoid extradition over Chilean kidnapping claims
item.description: A former Bondi nanny accused of torture and kidnapping for the Chilean Pinochet regime loses her final legal bid to avoid extradition to Chile.

item.title: Michael Voss blocking out Carlton criticism ahead of AFL Gather Round
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss insists he is ignoring most of the intense scrutiny on his embattled AFL club, adamant key stakeholders remain on the same page.

item.title: Woman charged over alleged murder of Pheobe Bishop to face court again in May
item.description: The case of a woman accused of murdering Queensland teenager Pheobe Bishop has been adjourned to May 15 — the first anniversary of Pheobe's disappearance.

item.title: At-home stockpiling sparks safety warnings from fuel and fire experts
item.description: Authorities are warning that improper or excessive storage of petrol or diesel in suburban homes carries a significant fire risk, as service station owners report excessive stockpiling.

item.title: 'Vegan leather' isn't as sustainable or eco‑friendly as brands might claim
item.description: As fashion brands move away from animal leather, "vegan" has come to suggest something that is both kinder to animals and better for the planet. However, the reality is more complicated.

item.title: NRL sends warning to Parramatta Eels captain Mitchell Moses for Easter Monday conduct
item.description: The NRL has warned Parramatta captain Mitch Moses to tone down his aggression when speaking to referees following the fiery golden-point loss to Wests Tigers on Easter Monday.

item.title: Rapper Offset — Cardi B's ex-husband — in stable condition after being shot
item.description: The Migos rapper is in a stable condition though his exact condition is unknown, according to his spokesperson.

item.title: Australia's EV sales surge in March amid growing fuel security fears, rising petrol prices
item.description: EV sales have surged in March, a month into the war in Iran. But it is not the only signal indicating a move away from fuel cars.

item.title: Simulated Artemis II Lunar Flyby
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Artemis II lunar fly-by breaks Apollo 13's distance record, reveals previously unseen far side of the Moon
item.description: The astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before, capturing never-before-seen views of the far side of the Moon.

item.title: Tourism sector experiences mixed results over Easter period, amid fuel price surge
item.description: It was a mixed weekend for many tourism businesses across the country, amid fears that rising fuel costs would keep people home. For some, it was business as usual, but fuel uncertainty has many in the sector feeling nervous about the months ahead.

item.title: Donald Trump's shifting rhetoric on Iran war works against his rapidly thinning credibility
item.description: Donald Trump's increasingly erratic language means that a US president's word — which would have once been regarded with gravitas — holds perhaps only the same weight as the word of the "evil" regime he says he has obliterated several times.

item.title: Canberra man jailed over 'substantially important' role in major illegal steroid and prescription drug syndicate
item.description: A Canberra man is jailed for his part in an illegal drug ring which operated as an online pharmacy, distributing steroids and prescription drugs across the country.

item.title: Aboriginal leaders Naomi Mayers and Ann Weldon remembered as trailblazers after dying days apart
item.description: Naomi Mayers helped establish the first Aboriginal medical services in Australia, turning self-determination into a model of care across the country. Her death came days before Wiradjuri woman and "fierce trailblazer" Ann Weldon.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos name Reece Walsh replacement, Victor Radley returns from suspension
item.description: Jesse Arthars has been named at fullback for the Brisbane Broncos ahead of Friday's blockbuster clash against the North Queensland Cowboys.

item.title: Talia Gibson jumps at chance to spearhead Australia against Britain in Billie Jean King Cup
item.description: Talia Gibson did not have to be asked twice to spearhead Australia's Billie Jean King Cup campaign against Great Britain, with the rising star happily putting the green and gold above her personal ambitions.

item.title: Townsville counting costs after crime spree sees nine arrested and police injured
item.description: Daniel Toppi was just starting to get back on his feet before a group of teenagers allegedly stole his work vehicle. Police have arrested nine teenagers in relation to the incident that also included the alleged theft of a police vehicle.

item.title: Police bodycam footage after nine people were arrested
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Friends watch search for rock fisherman missing from NSW mid north coast
item.description: Friends watched helplessly as a 32-year-old man was washed off Emerald Beach Headland, unable to swim to his rescue.

item.title: Australian War Memorial to review Ben Roberts-Smith display following war crimes charges
item.description: The Australian War Memorial will review its display of Ben Roberts-Smith, who has been charged with committing war crimes.

item.title: Police allege P-plater was driving 54km over speed limit on beach over Easter
item.description: Police crack down on beach driving these school holidays — alleging two minors were driving unlicensed and a P-plater was driving 54km/h over the speed limit in a 40km/h zone.

item.title: Big Banana graffiti investigated as possible act of hate speech under new laws
item.description: The graffiti, criticising Israel, was sprayed onto Coffs Harbour's famous Big Banana over the Easter long weekend.

item.title: Maree Mavis Crabtree pleads not guilty to murdering son Jonathan with spiked fruit smoothie
item.description: Maree Mavis Crabtree has pleaded not guilty to the murder of her son Jonathan in 2017, his attempted murder earlier that year, and attempted fraud.

item.title: Wallabies player Josh Flook speaks about move to Italy after Super Rugby Pacific season
item.description: Josh Flook has elaborated on the lifelong criticism that prompted his move to Italy and defended incoming Wallabies coach Les Kiss as crunch time looms for the Queensland Reds.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Netball ahead of the curve on sports gambling and the pending ownership of the Giants
item.description: The ex-CEO of Netball Australia said it was behind the times when she walked out the door in December 2023, but new gambling reforms announced by the federal government show the sport was right to steer clear from sports betting.

item.title: Man sentenced over fatal hit and run of teenager Bryson Dimovski near Newcastle
item.description: The father of a teenager who was fatally struck while riding an e-scooter near Newcastle says the consequences of driving while distracted are catastrophic.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: AI supercharging online scams as regulator ASIC takes down almost 12,000 sites in a year
item.description: Almost 12,000 scam websites have been shut down by Australia's financial regulator in a year as it continues a "cat-and-mouse game" with cybercriminals.

item.title: Man accused of three-day armed carjacking spree across southern Queensland appears in court
item.description: Police allege Beau Wallace Flegler, 39, stole multiple vehicles at gunpoint across the Easter long weekend before his arrest in East Toowoomba.

item.title: POLAIR vision of the arrest of 39-year-old man following a carjacking spree
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner facing drink driving charges in Sydney
item.description: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner is charged with mid-range drink driving in Sydney's east over the weekend.

item.title: Artemis II on record-setting moon flyby mission
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Fiji flight from Sydney diverted after tropical cyclone thwarts three landing attempts
item.description: A Fiji Airways flight from Sydney had to divert after bad weather from a tropical cyclone thwarted its attempts to land.

item.title: Kaylee McKeown, Lani Pallister and Kyle Chalmers claim wins at Australian Open swimming championships
item.description: Bugs swarm the Gold Coast Aquatic Centre pool as Australia's best and brightest swimming talents showed their versatility on day two of the Australian Open championships.

item.title: Kanye West blocked from entering UK to headline music festival after outcry from Jewish leaders
item.description: The British government's decision forces London's Wireless festival to cancel the event.

item.title: Gunman killed, two police officers injured in shooting outside Israeli consulate in Türkiye
item.description: A gunman has been killed and two people injured during an attack outside the Israeli consulate in Türkiye.

item.title: International baby trafficking syndicate charged in Indonesia
item.description: Babies, some as young as three months, were allegedly sold to people in Singapore and across Indonesia.

item.title: Ceasefire on brink of collapse as Iran threatens to retaliate for Israeli strikes on Beirut — as it happened
item.description: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon after Benjamin Netanyahu said its northern neighbour was not included in the ceasefire deal agreed by the US and Iran.

item.title: Trump agrees to 'double-sided ceasefire' with Iran less than 90 minutes before deadline
item.description: Donald Trump says he has agreed to suspend US attacks on Iran for two weeks as part of a "double-sided ceasefire". Iran is claiming victory and says it will stop its attacks if the US does.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 8: Pack your suitcase, we're going on a trip
item.description: Which European microstate is smaller? And which US president isn’t on Mount Rushmore?

item.title: Sword of the Sea is the Grammy Award-winning follow-up from the team behind Journey
item.description: Art director Matt Nava and composer Austin Wintory reveal how their cult 2012 video game Journey shaped their latest offering, the Grammy Award-winning Sword of the Sea.

item.title: How Australia's most decorated soldier, Ben Roberts-Smith, ended up facing war crime charges
item.description: During his years-long battle to clear his name, a separate joint investigation stretching 9,000 kilometres was discreetly transpiring.

item.title: Elite women athletes question their future in Australian sport as pay lags behind expectations
item.description: Recently retired rugby union player Mel Kawa regularly broke down in tears, grappling with her love of the game and the $2,000 she was paid to play it.

item.title: Boards make decisions affecting the nation but they don't look like Australia
item.description: There's a push to get younger people, women and more diverse community members on the boards making the big decisions in Australian life.

item.title: The grief of being estranged from family
item.description: Estrangement usually begins after a significant traumatic experience with a loved one, experts say.

item.title: NDIS provider registrations in spotlight as Labor eyes options to make budget savings
item.description: Stricter registration rules would weed out low-quality NDIS providers while delivering better services and budget savings, major operators say.

item.title: PM urged to intervene in youth justice systems to break the cycle of crime
item.description: Hundreds of legal, health and children's organisations are calling on Anthony Albanese to convene an emergency summit to address the over-incarceration of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children.

item.title: Cost of filling up Australia's best-selling car increases by $100 since start of Iran war
item.description: The ABC analyses how the cost of a full tank in Australia's most-purchased cars of 2025 has changed since the beginning of the Iran war.

item.title: Myanmar's women caught between war, disaster and blocked aid
item.description: Women who had already lost their homes to last year's earthquake are now also cut off from essentials like sanitary pads and aid.

item.title: Matt Canavan's 'Australia on steroids' economic plan looks to the stars, manufacturing and a baby boom
item.description: A domestic space industry, a baby boom, new dams and more factories are on Nationals leader Matt Canavan's to-do list to deliver what he describes as an Australian economic revolution.

item.title: National park expansion fuels land price surge and anxiety of NSW farmers
item.description: Hailed as a win for the environment, $200 million has been spent purchasing new national parks in outback NSW — but not everyone is celebrating.

item.title: Child safety legislation change ensures mandatory child abuse reporting from Tasmania Police
item.description: The child safety regulator sought a law change after receiving "concerning" correspondence from Tasmania Police, which said its officers were not bound by a key child protection measure.

item.title: Category five Cyclone Maila slowly moving towards Far North Queensland, tourists urged not to cancel travel plans
item.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is now a category five system and is expected to cross into Far North Queensland on Sunday.

item.title: Queensland's seller disclosure laws leaving some buyers 'gazumped', real estate experts warn
item.description: Real estate experts say Queensland's seller disclosure laws, which were introduced in August, are fuelling 'gazumping' and leading to unnecessary costs.

item.title: Gippsland farmers angered by plans to turn Hazelwood coal mine into huge lake
item.description: There are plans to turn the former Hazelwood coal mine in Victoria into a lake bigger than Sydney Harbour, but farmers and environmentalists are worried about the impact on nearby waterways.

item.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Middle East on the brink as Trump's deadline is hours away
item.description: Warning sirens are sounding and preparations are being made as the US and Iran ratchet up the threats against each other.

item.title: SA authorities confirm Port Lincoln tuna deaths not caused by harmful algal bloom
item.description: Environmental authorities have found the culprit for a mass fish mortality event off the coast of Port Lincoln, saying it is unrelated to the state's harmful algal bloom.

item.title: Darwin midwife's $1.1 million NT government contract under review
item.description: Darwin midwife Claire Marks was under investigation by the nursing and midwifery watchdog when she was handed a $1.1 million contract by the NT government, which is now reviewing the matter, as doctors continue to raise concerns, and the service brings on new staff.

item.title: Alex de Minaur defeats Cameron Norrie at Monte Carlo Masters round of 32
item.description: Alex de Minaur is through to the final 16 of the Monte-Carlo Masters after defeating Britain's Cameron Norrie.

item.title: Donald Trump sends JD Vance to save far-right Hungarian PM Viktor Orbán
item.description: US President Donald Trump has sent his vice-president to Hungary in a bid to salvage the political future of far-right leader Viktor Orbán, who is flailing in the polls days before a closely watched election.

item.title: Perth parents found guilty of starving teenage daughter granted bail ahead of possible retrial
item.description: A Perth man and woman who were found guilty in 2024 of starving their daughter, who weighed about 28 kilograms when she was admitted to hospital aged nearly 17, have their convictions set aside on appeal.

item.title: Hobby beekeepers urged to check for varroa mite as more than 1,000 ACT beehives destroyed
item.description: Experts say half of the ACT's 6,000 beehives have been impacted by the fast-spreading parasite varroa mite with more than 1,000 destroyed.

item.title: Phillip Norris walks on new leg
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Leg amputee forced to wait two months for rehabilitation services in Tamworth
item.description: After travelling more than 2,000 kilometres across four separate hospitals, Phillip Norris says staff shortages in hospitals "needs to change".

item.title: 'We've been calling for a de-escalation': PM welcomes ceasefire — as it happened
item.description: Anthony Albanese has described the two-week US-Iran ceasefire as positive news but says Donald Trump's rhetoric overnight wasn't appropriate.

item.title: Bryson DeChambeau hoping to continue rivalry with Rory McIlroy at US Masters
item.description: Bryson DeChambeau has fired the first verbal shot at Rory McIlroy as golf's alpha males prepare to collide in a compelling 90th Masters at Augusta National.

item.title: ASX enjoys $80 billion rally, oil falls back below $US100 on US-Iran ceasefire — as it happened
item.description: The news of a US-Iran ceasefire in the Perisan Gulf sparked a big rally on Asian share markets, with the ASX up almost 3 per cent while oil prices plummeted.

item.title: Jewellery shop targeted in second alleged arson on Gold Coast in two days
item.description: The latest incident comes a day after a car was allegedly rammed into a Gold Coast car wash business and set alight.

item.title: Sydney healthcare workers push for refugee Medicare access amid growing health concerns
item.description: A growing number of people seeking asylum in Australia are missing out on lifesaving preventative health care due to being ineligible for Medicare, a group of Sydney doctors and nurses say.

item.title: Carlton Blues player Will Hayward to miss Gather Round match against Adelaide Crows
item.description: Carlton will be without Will Hayward for a high-stakes clash against Adelaide after failing to downgrade his one-match ban for striking.

item.title: Man found dead at property in Victoria's north-west during missing person investigation
item.description: Police have discovered the body of a man on a property east of Ouyen in regional Victoria.

item.title: Swimming Australia's support dog experiment wins plenty of fans at Australian Open
item.description: The swimmers at the Australian Open championships this week have had additional support from the side of the pool in the form of three retriever dogs.

item.title: Queensland premier calls for fast-track of oil projects amid fuel crisis
item.description: Queensland Premier David Crisafulli wants the federal government to fast-track environmental approvals for oil projects.

item.title: Squid sightings spawn hope in SA amid calls for longer-lasting restrictions
item.description: Sightings of southern calamari in South Australia are bringing relief after the species all but disappeared from some areas during the harmful algal bloom that began in early 2025.

item.title: Mitch Duke to retire from Socceroos after FIFA World Cup bid
item.description: Mitch Duke will retire from international football this year, but is still hopeful of being part of Australia's FIFA World Cup squad.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith does not apply for bail in first court hearing on war crime charges
item.description: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith will remain in custody after his lawyers made no application for bail in his first court hearing since his arrest.

item.title: Tiny town of Cooladdi for sale as owners call time at Foxtrap Roadhouse
item.description: Cooladdi, nine hours west of Brisbane, dwindled in population when its railway station closed, but it continues to attract road-trippers and graziers. Its owners run local services including a shop, motel and mail service.

item.title: Nursing union says Royal Darwin Hospital staff face violent threats, fear for safety
item.description: NT Health has rejected calls for a security review at Royal Darwin Hospital's busy emergency department amid threats against staff, with staff concerned "a worker is going to be stabbed".

item.title: First person charged under Queensland hate speech laws faces court
item.description: Liam Parry, 33, was arrested and charged last month for reciting the banned phrase "from the river to the sea."

item.title: Water storage feud heats up as frustrated Central Queensland farmers take on corporate giants
item.description: Central Queensland irrigators are demanding a "fair go" from two mega water users that are pumping from a ratepayer-owned storage without paying anything for its multimillion-dollar costs.

item.title: Comedian Jenny Tian is back in Australia with her show When Life Gives You Oranges
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Gary Woodland details PTSD struggles ahead of US Masters
item.description: In an interview before this week's US Masters, former US Open champion Gary Woodland opens up about his mental health struggles.

item.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire deal between the US and Iran
item.description: These are the key moments that lead up to a two-week ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran, and what is on the table to permanently end the fighting.

item.title: Lisa McCune stars in stage production of iconic 80s movie Steel Magnolias
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: What is financial stress and how does it affect our decisions?
item.description: If financial stress is causing distractions — for example, you're worrying about money when you should be concentrating on driving or work — there might be something deeper happening.

item.title: Trump's escalating rhetoric inappropriate for a United States president, Albanese says
item.description: Hours before Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, the United States president threatened that "a whole civilisation will die tonight, never to be brought back again".

item.title: Shayna Jack 'lighter' as she strives for Commonwealth Games selection
item.description: Shayna Jack says her Paris Olympics campaign was fuelled by "anger and hate", but she is back to being herself — "lighter" and with more personal belief than ever.

item.title: Ken Hinkley says he was shamed and exploited by AFL over controversial fine
item.description: Ex-Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley, who was fined for sledging in 2024, says he felt exploited by the league's later use of footage of his theatrics as a promotional tool.

item.title: Fuel crisis hearing told truck drivers on brink of 'going out of business' without immediate help
item.description: An emergency Fair Work Commission hearing in Sydney begins, with unions and employers in the transport industry saying drivers, employees and businesses are on the verge of going under, calling on the top of the supply chain to share the load.

item.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas pushes for longer-term deal for AFL Gather Round
item.description: The South Australian premier is pushing for the state to be awarded a long-term contract for Gather Round, but says it could take weeks before a deal about the future of the footy festival is struck.

item.title: Three-day hearing set for Grace Tame abuser Nicolaas Bester over alleged 'menacing' social media posts
item.description: Nicolaas Bester, who was previously found guilty of sexually abusing Grace Tame, has been deemed unfit to stand trial over allegations he made "menacing" posts directed at her on social media.

item.title: Federal government criticised for response into spread of sea urchins
item.description: Millions of destructive sea urchins are spreading from NSW into Victoria and Tasmania and the chair of a Senate inquiry says reefs are at risk unless there is decisive action.

item.title: Mistrial declared in case of alleged murder victim Geoffrey McLean, whose remains were found in wheelie bin
item.description: A trial of four defendants charged in connection to the alleged murder of Geoffrey McLean — whose body was found dumped in a wheelie bin in Adelaide's north — has been vacated after a juror conducted independent research.

item.title: The Testaments, sequel to The Handmaid's Tale, arrives with similarly eerie timing
item.description: The Handmaid's Tale sequel, The Testaments, focuses on young women being groomed for marriage at an elite preparatory school headed by Aunt Lydia in Gilead.

item.title: SA farmers gamble on early winter crops after record rain, but expensive fuel and fertiliser a big concern
item.description: Some SA farmers have rolled the dice on planting winter crops weeks earlier than usual thanks to record-breaking rains. Now they hope the gamble pays off.

item.title: Kyogle train derailment happened on track with history of defects, report into NSW crash finds
item.description: An investigation into the derailment of a freight train in northern NSW has found it happened on a section of track with a history of "geometry defects".

item.title: Strathfield Council plan to rename Homebush slammed for chasing 'snob value'
item.description: Some councillors think this area gets confused with Sydney's Olympic precinct and should be renamed. But the plan using the neighbouring suburb's name has rankled some in the community.

item.title: Driver seriously injured, Princes Freeway shut for hours after truck collision at Officer
item.description: The crash shutdown outbound lanes of the Princes Freeway at Officer in Melbourne's south-east.

item.title: The three reasons behind Penrith's record-breaking start to the 2026 NRL season
item.description: Already, the 2026 premiership looks like it's Penrith's to lose, and there are three big reasons behind the Panthers' record-breaking start to the season.

item.title: A Good Friday football match between the Macedon Cats and Gisborne Giants turns ugly.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police investigate alleged on-field punch that left Victorian footballer with broken jaw
item.description: Victoria Police is investigating an on-field incident during a Good Friday football clash in the Macedon Ranges that left one player with a broken jaw and sparked a melee.

item.title: When is the best time to get your flu shot? Two infectious diseases experts explain
item.description: When working out the best time to get your vaccine, you may be balancing a theoretical benefit by waiting against the chance of actually getting the flu before you get vaccinated.

item.title: The far side revealed: Artemis II's lunar fly-by in pictures
item.description: A record-breaking voyage, an earthset in silence, and a total solar eclipse — astronauts on the Artemis II mission experienced sights no human has witnessed in half a century.

item.title: Drunk driver jailed over hit-and-run death which led to changes in WA road laws
item.description: An intoxicated driver who killed a woman in a hit-and-run in Mandurah, and was allowed to drive two months later, is sentenced to more than four years in prison.

item.title: Donald Trump cedes ground to Iran before negotiations even begin
item.description: By defining the framework set out by Iran as "workable", the US president has ceded ground before negotiations have even begun.

item.title: Coroner hears of police failings following 1976 disappearance of Veronica Green
item.description: Ms Green was last seen alive on February 13, 1976, with the Coroner's Court of Victoria hearing the litany of policing errors following her disappearance.

item.title: Three-bin FOGO waste on shaky ground in WA's South West amid rising diesel prices
item.description: Western Australia's second largest city is investigating the viability of the FOGO waste system amid rising costs, with more than a year's worth of waste trucked to landfill.

item.title: Bones discovered during bus stop construction work on Rottnest Island, sparking investigation
item.description: Police are investigating the origin of what are believed to be several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island.

item.title: Jackie O complained about Kyle Sandilands months before on-air fight, court documents allege
item.description: Court documents allege Jackie "O" Henderson was subject to "offensive and degrading" comments about her personal and dating life from co-host Kyle Sandilands in two separate incidents last year.

item.title: Pet dog survives crocodile attack at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley
item.description: A pet dog has a lucky escape after being dragged underwater by a freshwater crocodile at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley.

item.title: St John Ambulance NT reaches 'critical system failure' for second time in a week, calls for more resources
item.description: The Northern Territory's ambulances service has reached "critical system failure" for the second time in a week, as a St John NT director says the service is so under-resourced its operations are at risk of failure "all the time".

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: More Tasmanians using public transport
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Free public transport luring thousands more Tasmanians onto buses
item.description: The temporary ability to catch public transport for free is turning some Tasmanians into "converts", while others are using it for new experiences.

item.title: Queensland Rail extends planned train track closures citing protected industrial action by union workers
item.description: Train commuters across Brisbane will be impacted by an additional four days of track closures with Queensland Rail citing protected industrial action by union workers as the reason for the extension.

item.title: Australia to remain in talks on action in the Strait of Hormuz as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles says discussions will continue about Australia's involvement in ensuring the Strait of Hormuz remains open.

item.title: Foreign Minister Penny Wong wants ceasefire to apply to Israel's military action in Lebanon
item.description: Australia's Foreign Minister Penny Wong has rebuked US President Donald Trump's threats and called for Israel to cease its military action in Lebanon.

item.title: DPP considering appeal of Dennis Naroldol's four-year sentence in Darwin domestic violence manslaughter case
item.description: A four-year prison term handed to a man who fatally stabbed in his wife on the Darwin Esplanade in 2023 could be appealed by prosecutors.

item.title: Trump announces two-week ceasefire with Iran
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Foreign Minister Penny Wong says the ceasefire should include Israel's action in Lebanon.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'We are so thankful': Perth man pays for others' petrol amid fuel price surge
item.description: WA construction company owner Monty Van der Berg wants to ease the cost of living crisis for Australians, by paying for their fuel one tank at a time.

item.title: Sam Williamson, Mollie O'Callaghan, Kaylee McKeown, Lani Pallister, Sam Short claim wins at Australian Open swimming championships
item.description: Sam Williamson capped his comeback with victory in the 50m breaststroke just 11 months after he suffered a career-threatening injury.

item.title: Hundreds killed in 'barbaric' wave of Israeli strikes in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli and Lebanese media are citing the Lebanese Red Cross in reporting the death toll could be as high as 300, with many more injured.

item.title: US architect Rex Heuermann admits to murder of eight women in Gilgo Beach case
item.description: Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to seven murders and admits to the killing of another woman, solving a notorious case known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

item.title: Live: Ceasefire on brink after Israel pounds Lebanon with wave of strikes
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu said Lebanon was not included in the truce, while Iran accused Israel of ceasefire breaches and barred ships from crossing the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.
